title: Glandulifolia mundiifolia
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called Glandulifolia mundiifolia. However, I can provide information on the plant Glandularia (Glandulifolia) as it might be the same plant, but it's more commonly known as Glandularia. 

Glandularia, also known as Glandulifolia, is a genus of flowering plants in the verbena family (Verbenaceae). The health benefits of Glandularia include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Glandularia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
